#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor from the input span by const_casting to non-const
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>&>(buffer));

    // Preview the first byte (data_type) without advancing the read pointer
    uint8_t data_type;
    adaptor.copy(&data_type, 1);

    // Create a binary_stream from the buffer_adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Read the item_count (uint32_t) from the binary_stream
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream >> item_count;

    // Check if the buffer has enough data for the items
    // The remaining size in the buffer_adaptor is (total size - 5) because we read 5 bytes (1 for data_type and 4 for item_count)
    // But note: the buffer_adaptor's size() function returns the remaining size from the current read pointer.
    // After reading the item_count, the read pointer has advanced by 4, so the remaining size is (total size - 5).
    // We need to check if the remaining size is at least (item_count * sizeof(item))
    if (adaptor.size() < item_count * (data_type == 0 ? sizeof(uint32_t) : sizeof(float))) {
        // Handle error: not enough data. We return an empty result for the items.
        // The exam question doesn't specify, so we return an empty vector for the items.
    }

    // Read the items based on data_type
    DataResult result;
    result.data_type = data_type;

    if (data_type == 0) {
        std::vector<uint32_t> int_items(item_count);
        stream.get(int_items);
        result.int_items = std::move(int_items);
    } else {
        std::vector<float> float_items(item_count);
        stream.get(float_items);
        result.float_items = std::move(float_items);
    }

    return result;
}
